title: pellaea trichophylla
intro: Pellaea trichophylla, also known as the hairy lip fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Pteridaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Pellaea trichophylla include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Pellaea trichophylla is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
